setting.note: The smooth swindler Chichikov travels the Russian provinces buying up 'dead souls', serfs who have died but still count on the tax rolls, in a scheme to mortgage them for a fortune, and in doing so exposes a whole gallery of grotesque landowners. Gogol maps a satirical but recognisable Russia: the provincial town where Chichikov charms the officials, the estates of the misers and dreamers he visits, and the endless road along which his carriage rolls.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Nikolai Gogol (d. 1852), first published 1842 (Myortvye dushi). Mapped as a cited work: we store no text and reproduce no passages here. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. This entry reproduces no text; the public-domain original is widely available.

palette.note: The tarnished gold of Chichikov's dream of easy riches, the acquisitive glitter Gogol turns into a mirror for a whole society's moral deadness. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
